beats.title: Omens at the Chasm and the Sudden Fall
beats.boundaryReason: Session setup and world prologue give way to the party awakening on the ice platform.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets drStart and drEnd to 1730-01-25
DM states 'It's now January 22nd' only within the backstory recap before play
Episode 1 note places the party's arrival on 1730-01-25, early afternoon

beats.title: Strangers Wake on the Ice
beats.boundaryReason: The party surveys the platform, introduces themselves, and investigates the environment until Eolo provokes the wolf guardians.
dateEvidence: DM says before the fall it was 'like, 3 o'clock in the afternoon'
No rest, sleep, or next-day transition occurs after the awakening

beats.title: Ice Wolves Spring to Life
beats.boundaryReason: Combat begins when Eolo touches the wolves, and this first phase centers on the initial wolf assault before shadow-creatures fully join the battle.
dateEvidence: This fight follows directly from the same 3 PM platform scene
No narrative sign of a day change or substantial elapsed downtime appears during the first combat phase

beats.title: Shades Descend on the Platform
beats.boundaryReason: The encounter escalates when wind-shades arrive, the Zimkovan woman drops, and the first dead wolf releases chasm wisps that change the fight.
dateEvidence: This beat is contiguous with the same ongoing platform combat on 1730-01-25
No sleep, long rest, sunrise, or other day-transition cue interrupts the battle

beats.title: Darkness, Wisps, and the Final Cleanup
beats.boundaryReason: After the first wisp is destroyed, the fight shifts into a final close-quarters cleanup in darkness until every remaining enemy is dead.
dateEvidence: This remains the same uninterrupted combat sequence on the session's start date
No evidence suggests a day or major time-of-day transition before the field is cleared

beats.title: Kalima Names the Labyrinth
beats.boundaryReason: With the fight over, Finnan and Justas stabilize the woman and extract her first coherent account of Zeyfa's Labyrinth and her people's history.
dateEvidence: Kalima says 'The sun must have risen by now,' implying the same day is still in progress
No overnight rest occurs between combat end and this conversation

beats.title: The Party Trades Histories
beats.boundaryReason: The focus shifts from Kalima's testimony to the party's own introductions, motives, and theories about whether this place is truly the chasm.
dateEvidence: Conversation continues seamlessly from Kalima's revelations on the same date
No line indicates sleeping, waking, or a move into the next day

beats.title: Short Rest and Dangerous Reconnaissance
beats.boundaryReason: The group questions Kalima about sacrifices, takes a short rest, probes the platform's exits, and confirms the wind below is deadly while Rala scouts the bridge ahead.
dateEvidence: DM explicitly says the party may as well take a short rest
Kalima notes 'The sun must have risen by now' and there is still no sunset or next-day cue
A short rest advances time within the same day rather than to a new date

beats.title: Kalima Agrees to Go In
beats.boundaryReason: After scouting reveals four tunnels, the party persuades Kalima to stay between Finnan and Justas and commits to entering the labyrinth.
dateEvidence: This decision follows immediately after the same short-rest scene and scouting report
The transcript ends before any sunset, sleep, or next-day transition
